common: Bark of an Indian Tree, introduced by Dr. ND Ray, Calcutta (JONOSIA ASOCA)

head.text: Unilateral headache; reflex uterine, congestive headache, better open air and by free flow. Pain in eyeballs; supraorbital pains, photophobia. Nasal catarrh, profuse, watery discharge. Loss of sense of smell

generalities.text: [Gastric] Desire for sweets, also acid things. Thirsty, excessive nausea; obstinate constipation, hæmorrhoids

female.text: Delayed and irregular menses; menstrual colic; amenorrhœa, pain in ovaries before flow; menorrhagia, irritable bladder; leucorrhœa

back.text: Pain along spine radiating to abdomen and thighs

keynotes.text: color="#800000">JOANESIA ASOCA Bark of an Indian Tree, introduced by Dr. ND Ray, Calcutta (JONOSIA ASOCA) Has extensive sphere of action on female organs. Amenorrhœa and metrorrhagia.
